mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and economic momentum, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which fuels short-term market optimism and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when INDPRO trends upward, or the signal reverses direction during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above the 3.0 percent threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tight and expanding labor market, supporting wage growth and consumer spending forecasts that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the relationship breaks down when payrolls grow but unemployment rises.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product figures confirm macroeconomic expansion and corporate earnings resilience, generating immediate positive sentiment that translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production exceeding one percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruption, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in out-of-sample validation.
